$comment: Single source of truth for the plugin stack. Edit this to add/remove plugins, then re-run bootstrap. 'type' is 'core' (shipped with Obsidian, only toggled on) or 'community' (downloaded from GitHub Releases). IDs and repos were resolved from obsidianmd/obsidian-releases; bootstrap re-resolves community IDs->repos from that registry at runtime and only falls back to the 'repo' field here if resolution fails.

corePlugins.name: Bases
corePlugins.note: Core plugin (ships with Obsidian). Enabled in core-plugins.json; never downloaded.

ai.$comment: Hybrid AI backends. bootstrap picks one per device and templates its host/model into the AI plugins' data.json. Default profile 'auto': use this device's local Ollama if it answers, else fall back to the rig. Override with --profile local|rig|auto. The rig is reached over Tailscale so it works from any device, including cellular. IMPORTANT: set backends.rig.host to your rig's Tailscale name (run `tailscale status` on the rig, or use its MagicDNS name / 100.x.y.z IP) — bootstrap refuses to target the rig while it is still the CHANGE-ME placeholder.
